calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-03-2026 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance or objection filed, the Court orders the case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Judge: Rochelle East; Clerk Z. Dizon; Proceedings not reported. (610)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-02-2026 Continued To Jun-02-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-13-2025 Continued To Mar-03-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Unopposed Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Final Judgment And Injunction
Off Calendar Oct-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Molly Alarcon Esq. Deputy City Attorney 415-554-3800 for Plaintiff CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO. (In-person)
Stacey Cooper, Esq. 619-673-5022 for Defendant Zen Compound.
Court reporter: Jane Hong, CSR #11975, 909-831-2644, csrjane@hotmail.com

ARGUED. Ms. Alarcon informs the court parties will proceed via the 2nd route. Court and Counsel discuss the issues. Ms. Alarcon requests to remove the paragraph on PAGA and class action statement from the court's ruling. Ms. Cooper seeks clarification on following the structure of a PAGA approval.

After argument, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Final Judgment And Injunction.

Plaintiff City and County of San Francsico's unopposed Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Final Judgment And Injunction is Taken Under Submission.***

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Jane Hong, CSR #11975, 909-831-2644, csrjane@hotmail.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an order denying without prejudice Plaintiff's unopposed motion for entry of stipulated final judgment and injunction. (see scanned order for details)***

Unopposed Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Final Judgment And Injunction
Off Calendar Oct-01-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff City and County of San Francsico's unopposed Motion For Entry Of Stipulated Final Judgment And Injunction is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

The motion is defective in several ways. First, the parties did not provide courtesy copies of their papers. (See LRSF, rule 2.7(B).) Second, the notice of motion itself cites no authority and no grounds for issuance of the order. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.1110(a) ["A notice of motion must state in the opening paragraph the nature of the order being sought and the grounds for issuance of the order."].) Third, the memorandum does not identify the applicable legal standard and explain why it is met on this record. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.1113(b) ["The memorandum [in support of a motion] must contain a statement of facts, a concise statement of the law, evidence and arguments relied on, and a discussion of the statutes, cases, and textbooks cited in support of the position advanced."].) Fourth, where parties move the court to execute a proposed judgment, the proposed judgment needs to be an exhibit in the motion and the merits of the request need to be explained; the key points of the judgment need to be summarized and discussed; where the proposed judgment includes an injunction, the parties need to explain why the court should enter and endorse such relief. Such may be obvious to the parties, but it needs to be explained to the court, which has no particular familiarity with this action.

The parties may file a new noticed motion correcting these deficiencies.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
